headline: Cyril Imhof's shorts converge on a single argument: cheaper models and agent integration are pushing enterprises to build in-house AI and abandon costly SaaS.
body: Imhof frames rising API prices [9] and falling SaaS economics [11] as the core pressure, with Chinese models cited for cost and data sovereignty advantages [4] and as the basis for self-built CRMs that replace established platforms [10]. He ties this to matching model complexity to task to maximize return on AI spend [12] and weighs Claude query costs against self-updating living artifacts in enterprise systems [13]. A second thread covers distribution and lock-in: MCPs as the mechanism for external agent access and recurring revenue [0][1], and an Anthropic vs Microsoft fight over Teams integration where Microsoft favors Copilot and is accused of EU monopoly dynamics [2][3]. He also notes the open-source gap closing from annual to monthly parity shifts [6] and Claude Tag accounting for 60% of internal Anthropic interactions as a digital-employee pattern [5][7][8].
